Script used to test os.kill on Windows, for issue #1220212
This script is started as a subprocess in test_os and is used to test the
CTRL_C_EVENT and CTRL_BREAK_EVENT signals, which requires a custom handler
to be written into the kill target.
See http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ms685049%28v=VS.85%29.aspx for a
similar example in C.
wintypes
WINFUNCTYPENc
z8Handle a sig event and return 0 to terminate the processz
UNKNOWN EVENTr
signalZ
CTRL_C_EVENTZ
CTRL_BREAK_EVENT
print)
0/usr/lib64/python3.9/test/win_console_handler.py
_ctrl_handler
__main__
z#Unable to add SetConsoleCtrlHandler
__doc__Z
ctypesr
mmap
sysZ
BOOLZ
DWORDZ
HandlerRoutiner
ctrl_handlerZ
windllZ
kernel32Z
SetConsoleCtrlHandler
argtypesZ
restype
__name__r
exit
argv
<module>
